33314415-10e1-810d-8c6e-cb767b72216b, 33314415-10e1-81bf-ae34-e7b54909e3f8, 33314415-10e1-8122-99cb-c36f6096cc2a, 33314415-10e1-81c8-8282-c3e844345ab1
network_affiliation
[[BioFi Project]]
type
organization
location_lat
8.5
background_section
## Background The Osa Peninsula stretches along Costa Rica's southern Pacific coast, a landscape that National Geographic has recognized as [one of the most biologically intense places on Earth](https://youtu.be/YPJ2Thbgbdc). This remarkable region contains [2.5% of the world's biodiversity and 5% of all plant and animal species](https://youtu.be/YPJ2Thbgbdc) within just three millionths of Earth's surface—a concentration of life that defies comprehension. The peninsula encompasses Corcovado National Park, the largest undeveloped stretch of lowland rainforest globally, Central America's largest Pacific coast mangrove forest, and the Gulf of Dulce, one of only four tropical fjords in the world. This ecological richness exists alongside Ngäbe Indigenous communities and Costa Rican cultures who have stewarded these lands for generations. Yet this extraordinary place faces mounting pressures: unsustainable development patterns, accelerating international land acquisition, poverty existing alongside conservation efforts, and cultural erosion. These challenges create vicious cycles where economic desperation drives illegal deforestation, poaching, and mining—activities that undermine the very ecosystems communities depend upon. ### Origins and Network The Centro Estratégico de Desarrollo de la Península de Osa (CEDPO) emerged from a decade-long collaboration between Regenerative Earth and local Osa Peninsula communities, formally established in [June 2022](https://youtu.be/Ip_SmDZgC2o). The organization represents a strategic response to the region's complex challenges, bringing together diverse actors around a shared vision for regenerative development.
Sources
33314415-10e1-81c0-a089-d94fa5e7f319, 33314415-10e1-81f9-bd3f-d37db6923636

Costa Rica
place
Osa Peninsula, Costa Rica
activities

description
Centro Estratégico de Desarrollo de la Península de Osa (CEDPO) is a collaborative platform working to unify local, Indigenous, and institutional actors around a shared vision for a regenerative future in Costa Rica's Osa Peninsula, rooted in system mapping, community governance, and ecological stewardship.
id
cedpo
vision_legacy_section
## Vision and Legacy CEDPO's three-year plan (2025-2027) includes establishing the Costa Rican Bioregional Financing Facility, building out the core operations team, and continuing strategic investments in projects that promote a regenerative future for the Osa Peninsula. The organization aims to ensure that development serves local communities while preserving the region's extraordinary ecological integrity, avoiding the extractive patterns seen elsewhere in Costa Rica. Osa’s challenges do not map neatly onto other regions: deep inequality, youth pulled into illicit economies for income, and tourism pressure that can repeat Guanacaste-style displacement when capital is foreign and skills stay imported. CEDPO points instead to **Arenal San Carlos**—locally owned hotels and restaurants—as the counter-pattern: build **capabilities** so residents lead development and keep value and stewardship in place.
website
https://regenerativeearth.org/osa-peninsula-regenerative-economy-lab/
bioregional_context
The Osa Peninsula, located on the southern Pacific coast of Costa Rica, is one of the most biodiverse regions on Earth. Home to roughly 2.5% of the planet's terrestrial species in less than 0.001% of its land area, it includes Corcovado National Park, extensive lowland rainforests, mangroves, and marine ecosystems.
category
Bioregional Organizer
